question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics, showing a monthly increase of only 0.3% on a seasonally adjusted basis. This is significantly below the 0.9% threshold required for the market to resolve YES. The data is official and final, making the probability of a YES resolution exactly 0%.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows 0.3% monthly CPI increase
Data is already released and final
No possibility of revision that could change outcome
risks: None - the data is officially released and conclusive
